message:
So last Tuesday I burned dinner—like, actually set off the smoke alarm. My first instinct was panic, but then I thought, 'Well, this is a good reminder anyway.' Instead of being annoyed, I made it a thing: turned off the stove, opened windows, and called my kids over to help me check our escape plan on paper. We walked through each room, talked about the nearest exits, and honestly? It felt less like a drill and more like a little adventure we were solving together.

By the end, they knew exactly where to go (and were giggling about how I somehow burned pasta), and I felt so much calmer knowing we'd actually thought it through out loud.

Have you and your family ever talked through a fire escape plan together? How did you make it feel natural instead of scary?
